title: Breaking Down The Best Picture Race Between "Sinners" And "One Battle After Another"
text: This analysis examines the 98th Academy Awards Best Picture competition between two films, drawing parallels to the historic 2016 Moonlight/La La Land race. Sinners secured four Academy Awards despite losing Best Picture. It earned wins in Best Original Screenplay, Best Original Score, Best Cinematography (Autumn Durald Arkapaw), and Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan), giving it a bigger Oscar haul than Moonlight, even without the top prize. One Battle After Another clinched six Oscars total, matching La La Land's count while winning Best Picture—an achievement La La Land failed to achieve. Its wins included Best Picture, Best Director, Best Supporting Actor, Best Original Screenplay, Best Casting, and Best Film Editing. Unlike 2016, when backlash against La La Land helped Moonlight prevail, criticisms of One Battle After Another never gained sufficient traction. The piece suggests that attempts to question the film's handling of racial and political themes never affected One Battle After Another for any extended period. The analysis concludes that 2025/26 differs significantly from 2016/17, with voters less inclined toward unconventional statements through Best Picture selections.
